---
title: "About"
---

I hold a B.Sc. in Mechanical Fluids Engineering and work in hydrology, hydraulics and water resources analysis. 

My research focuses on hydrological modelling, precipitation analysis, uncertainty assessment and environmental data science. I am particularly interested in understanding how data limitations, modelling choices and methodological assumptions influence hydrological predictions and decision-making in data-scarce environments.

My current research explores rainfall–runoff modelling, satellite and gridded precipitation products, extreme event analysis, and the application of statistical and machine learning methods in hydrology.

This website serves as a repository of publications, research projects, technical notes and reproducible workflows developed using R, GIS tools and open hydrological datasets.

## Research Interests

- Hydrology and Water Resources
- Hydrological Modelling
- Hydrological Frequency Analysis
- Extreme Rainfall and Flood Analysis
- Satellite and Gridded Precipitation Products
- Hydrological Uncertainty Assessment
- Machine Learning Applications in Hydrology
- Environmental Data Science
- Reproducible Research with R and Quarto

## Professional Profiles

- ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0009-0009-3386-8571>
- GitHub: <https://github.com/JamilRamirez>
- LinkedIn: <https://www.linkedin.com/in/jamil-r-762098b1/>

## Contact

For research collaborations, academic inquiries or professional opportunities, feel free to connect through the professional profiles listed above.
